breach.summary: The Upbound Group fintech company disclosed that threat actors who stole data from its systems leveraged it to create $13 million in Acima leases. [...]
breach.org: Upbound says hack caused $13 million in fraudulent Acima leases

breach.summary: South Korea disclosed that hackers breached the National Diplomatic Academy's online education system for ten months and stole personal information belonging to current and former employees of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA), including overseas diplomats. [...]
breach.org: South Korea discloses data breach impacting diplomats worldwide

breach.summary: Hackers leaked names, email addresses, phone numbers, passwords, and financial information stolen from the two platforms. The post Suno, Paidwork Data Breaches Affect Tens of Millions of Accounts appeared first on SecurityWeek.
breach.org: Suno, Paidwork Data Breaches Affect Tens of Millions of Accounts

breach.summary: An attacker only needed to convince the targeted user to visit a malicious website to exfiltrate WhatsApp messages and contacts. The post Flaw in Adobe Extension With 300M Installs Enabled WhatsApp Data Theft appeared first on SecurityWeek.
breach.org: Flaw in Adobe Extension With 300M Installs Enabled WhatsApp Data Theft

breach.summary: European banks inadvertently transmitted customer data to ad platforms via tracking pixels, raising serious compliance, security, and privacy concerns.
breach.org: EU Financial Institutions Leak Data Through Cookie Trackers

breach.summary: American fast food restaurant chain Chick-fil-A is notifying customers of a data breach after their accounts were hacked in a wave of recent credential stuffing attacks. [...]
breach.org: Chick-fil-A discloses data breach after credential stuffing attacks

cve.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in snap-confine that an unprivileged user can trigger to obtain root access and gain complete control of a target environment. The high-severity flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-8933 (CVSS score: 7.8), impacts default...
cve.title: Ubuntu snap-confine Flaw Could Give Local Users Root on Default Desktop Installs

cve.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched vulnerability chain in the Adobe Acrobat Chrome extension that has over 314 million users, which, if exploited, could facilitate a silent hijack of a user's WhatsApp data. The shortcoming has been codenamed HermeticReader by Guardio Labs. It's...
cve.title: Adobe Acrobat Extension Flaw Let Malicious Sites Read WhatsApp Web Data

cve.summary: A high-severity security flaw impacting open-source developer platform Windmill has come under active exploitation in the wild, per VulnCheck. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-29059 (CVSS score: 7.5), a case of unauthenticated path traversal impacting Windmill's "get_log_file" endpoint...
cve.title: Hackers Exploit Windmill Flaw to Read Arbitrary Server Files Without Authentication

cve.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday ordered U.S. government agencies to prioritize patching an actively exploited vulnerability in the Langflow visual framework for building AI agents. [...]
cve.title: CISA orders urgent action on actively exploited Langflow RCE flaw

cve.summary: CVE-2026-50522 is being exploited by threat actors to steal machine keys and retain long-term access. The post Fourth SharePoint Vulnerability Exploited in Past Month’s Wave of Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
cve.title: Fourth SharePoint Vulnerability Exploited in Past Month’s Wave of Attacks

cve.summary: Hackers are actively exploiting the critical CVE-2026-50522 vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint to steal machine keys and maintain access even after affected servers are patched. [...]
cve.title: Critical SharePoint RCE flaw exploited to steal machine keys

threat.summary: Sandworm_Mode is an early example of malware that exploits trusted AI tools and workflows to make malicious activity virtually indistinguishable from normal activity.
threat.actor: Attackers Are Learning to Live Off the AI Toolchain

threat.summary: A malicious application delivers four-stage Android spyware via phony Google Play sites, exploiting civilian fear during Iranian missile strikes.
threat.actor: Fake Bahrain Alert App Deploys Android Surveillance Malware

threat.summary: The cycle is over. For years, cybersecurity followed a familiar pattern: defenses improved, attackers adapted, and the back-and-forth continued. Today, AI-equipped attackers are simply outpacing defenses. Most intrusions now bypass endpoint and malware-based detection entirely. The CrowdStrike Global Threat Report...
threat.actor: Why Modern SOCs Need Multi-Layered Detections

threat.summary: Using AI, the startup provides adaptive prevention through environment mapping, risk analysis, and automated policy enforcement. The post Endpoint Security Firm Glow Launches With $180M in Funding at $1.2B Valuation appeared first on SecurityWeek.
threat.actor: Endpoint Security Firm Glow Launches With $180M in Funding at $1.2B Valuation

threat.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a NuGet typosquat that's unlike the typical information-stealing malware distributed via package registries: usual info-stealers: it's designed to rig live game results on Digitain. The package, named "Newtonsoftt.Json.Net," masquerades as the Newtonsoft.Json library and is...
threat.actor: Trojanized Newtonsoft.Json Fork Hides Game-Rigging Code in a Working Library

threat.summary: A large-scale operation dubbed 'FakeGit' is pushing SmartLoader and StealC malware through 7,600 malicious GitHub repositories that accumulated more than 14 million downloads. [...]
threat.actor: FakeGit campaign uses 7,600 GitHub repos to push SmartLoader malware

news.summary: OpenAI says its AI models, including GPT‑5.6 Sol and a pre-release model, hacked into the Hugging Face artificial intelligence repository while being tested in a sandboxed testing environment. [...]
news.title: OpenAI says its AI models hacked Hugging Face during testing

news.summary: Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent invite-only VIP tier will pay $30,000 or more. Reports filed before that date, including those already in GitHub's...
news.title: GitHub Cuts Public Bug Bounty Payouts, Moves Top Rewards to VIP Tier

news.summary: German and US law enforcement have taken down the core infrastructure of Kratos, described by German investigators as one of the world's most widely used criminal phishing kits, and Indonesian authorities arrested the man they say developed and ran it. In a joint announcement on Monday, the Frankfurt public...
news.title: Police Dismantle Kratos Phishing Kit Built to Steal Microsoft 365 Sessions and Bypass MFA

news.summary: Every patch is a confession. The moment a vendor ships a security fix, the diff between the old code and the new code tells anyone watching exactly what was broken and where. Turn that diff back into a working exploit, and you can hit every system that hasn't updated yet. This is N-day exploitation, and it's always...
news.title: N-day is Becoming N-Hour. Patching Faster Won't Save You.

news.summary: A cloud tenant using nothing but ordinary GPU access can push a data center's power draw up and down fast enough to threaten the grid it runs on, with no exploit and no break-in. That is the claim behind Bit2Watt, described by three Zhejiang University researchers in a paper accepted to CHES 2026, the IACR's...
news.title: New Bit2Watt Attack Could Let Cloud Tenants Disrupt Power Grids Without an Exploit

news.summary: Acquisition follows January's Chronosphere deal, deepening Palo Alto Networks' push beyond core security into observability. The post Palo Alto Networks to Acquire Observability Platform Provider Embrace appeared first on SecurityWeek.
news.title: Palo Alto Networks to Acquire Observability Platform Provider Embrace

news.summary: Identity confidence changes throughout every interaction and should be reassessed continuously as new risk signals emerge. The post When Identity Verification Fails: Lessons from a Real-World SIM Swap and Near Account Takeover appeared first on SecurityWeek.
news.title: When Identity Verification Fails: Lessons from a Real-World SIM Swap and Near Account Takeover

news.summary: The startup will use the fresh investment to accelerate its go-to-market strategy and to expand its platform. The post StrongestLayer Raises $4.1 Million in Seed Funding Extension appeared first on SecurityWeek.
news.title: StrongestLayer Raises $4.1 Million in Seed Funding Extension

news.summary: Eclypsium has launched InfraTrust, a new infrastructure cybersecurity knowledge base and monthly InfraTrust Pulse report designed to help organizations prioritize vulnerabilities affecting infrastructure, firmware, networking, and edge devices. [...]
news.title: New InfraTrust report reveals infrastructure flaws admins should patch first

news.summary: The Adobe Acrobat extension for Chrome could be used to access conversations and data rendered in WhatsApp Web without any form of authentication. [...]
news.title: Adobe Chrome extension flaw let sites access private WhatsApp chats

news.summary: Authorities in Germany and the U.S. dismantled the central infrastructure of Kratos, a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform with global reach, and its developer was arrested in Indonesia. [...]
news.title: Police dismantle Kratos phishing platform, arrest developer

news.summary: Security leaders who build fast, visible paths to AI adoption are becoming the most valued partners in their organizations. AI governance done right gives security teams the visibility they need, employees the tools they want, and CISOs the strategic influence they have earned. According to McKinsey's State of AI...
news.title: The Fastest Path to AI Adoption Runs Through Security

kev.action: Apply mitigations in accordance with vendor instructions, ensuring compliance with CISA’s BOD 26-04 Prioritizing Security Updates Based on Risk (see URL in Notes) guidance and CISA’s “Forensics Triage Requirements” (see URL in Notes). Follow applicable BOD 26-04 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable. Stakeholders are responsible for evaluating each asset's internet exposure and ensuring adherence to BOD 26-04 patching guidelines.

patch.summary: Microsoft has reminded customers that it will stop shipping security updates for Exchange 2016 and 2019 through the Extended Security Update (ESU) program in October. [...]
patch.title: Microsoft to stop Exchange 2016 / 2019 security updates in October

patch.summary: Many of the vulnerabilities fixed with the July 2026 Critical Patch Update were likely discovered by AI. The post Oracle Patches Over 1,400 Vulnerabilities With Quarterly Security Updates appeared first on SecurityWeek.
patch.title: Oracle Patches Over 1,400 Vulnerabilities With Quarterly Security Updates

ransomware.summary: A cyberattack on a food and logistics firm disrupts the supply of frozen food to thousands of clients, including major franchises like Kentucky Fried Chicken.
ransomware.group: Ransomware Attack Puts a Chill On Japanese Frozen-Food Chain

ransomware.summary: Swiss rail vehicle manufacturer Stadler Rail says the Everest ransomware gang demanded about $12.3 million after breaching a data exchange platform shared with one of its suppliers. [...]
ransomware.group: Swiss rail giant Stadler rejects $12.3M ransom demand after cyberattack

ransomware.summary: Enterprise AI can accelerate ransomware attacks when AI assistants and agents inherit excessive permissions or compromised identities. Acronis explains how identity controls, governance, and least-privilege access help reduce AI-enabled ransomware risk while supporting secure AI adoption. [...]
ransomware.group: How enterprise GenAI can amplify ransomware risk — and how to contain it

ransomware.summary: The Anubis ransomware group claims to have stolen 1 TB of confidential data from the Coca-Cola subsidiary. The post Ransomware Group Threatening to Leak Data Stolen From Coca-Cola’s Fairlife appeared first on SecurityWeek.

ransomware.summary: Researchers pointed to fragmentation of the ransomware ecosystem, the emergence of new attackers, and expansion of attacks on less defended organizations.
